conflicting_findings: Psychosis involves dysregulation of multiple neurotransmitters.
Dopamine is not the only neurotransmitter linked to psychosis.
Pimavanserin is a new treatment for Parkinson's disease psychosis with serotonin 2A antagonist properties.
New atypical antipsychotics are effective against negative and cognitive symptoms of schizophrenia and affective symptoms of depression.
The review provides an overview of the neurobiological underpinnings of schizophrenia and depression.
5-HT2A and mGlu2/3 receptors co-localize but do not physically associate.
Activation of mGlu2/3 receptors inhibits glutamate exocytosis.
Blockade of 5-HT2A receptors enhances the inhibitory effect of mGlu2/3 receptor agonists.
dominant_mechanisms: Dopamine hyperactivity in the mesolimbic pathway
summary:
The documents at hand commonly cover schizophrenia. Across these documents, dominant 
mechanisms include Dopamine hyperactivity in the mesolimbic pathway, NMDA receptor hypofunction, 5-HT2A receptor hyperfunction. 
Key consensus: no clear consensus.
